here.

it, man.

then.

Right.

that's great.

So um always good to get a little bit of a refresher.

Yeah, it's definitely been um I don't just, you know, Thanksgiving through New Year is always kind of um just a wild ride.

It's, it's, you know, it's uh things don't follow kind of our normal trends and um it's always a little bit tougher for, for the teams, but I feel like we, I started seeing just this week that we're kind of pulling back into um definitely a slower season here in Ohio.
Uh So we're in Ohio and Florida and Ohio gets harder, hit harder with that seasonality.
So things are definitely a little bit slower then during peak season, but they're starting to kind of come back up to like um normal levels for, for this time of year.
Um Florida definitely gets impacted by holidays and things, but we're seeing a little bit less of that.
Um, you know, it, February in Ohio, nobody wants to move.
So, usually December through March are a lot slower here in Ohio.
Whereas, you know, sunshine in Florida, it's not quite as big an issue.
So,
has been unseasonably cold this year though.
So, maybe that's slowing it down a little bit as well.
Ok.
So how are you guys looking occupancy wise then as you stayed pretty consistent there or are there any properties you'd say that kind of keep you up at night that are running a little bit behind?
Um We are overall pretty stable across the board.
We've got a couple of communities that are going through either a lease op.

We've got a couple that are going through um some sort of like repositioning and stabilization and things like that.
So we've got a couple that are kind of getting uh getting all of the, you know, pricing and things like that kind of in the right spot.
Um And then some that are, are a little bit more set?
Ok.

And the Columbus, Columbus's downtown market is just super saturated with um very, you know, high end luxury apartments right now with more coming online.
So I think that it's very much a, a pricing and competition issue as opposed to a exposure and awareness issue.

and then we've got some more like grassroots stuff that we do.

listing.

But even just kind of the year in general 2023 has kind of been seeing things go back to like pre COVID times when it comes to occupancy.
We've been seeing a lot more challenges when it comes to filling units.

So, um, some nuances by market and asset class.
But yeah, I think overall it's pretty accurate statement.

Our A I platform will actually monitor that success and see where you guys are getting most leads and quality of leads from for each property individually.

Obviously, your Jackson 1 may perform a little different than Columbus.
